A **command** in [military terminology](/wiki/Military_terminology "Military terminology") is an organisational unit for which a [military commander](/wiki/Commanding_officer "Commanding officer") is responsible. Commands, sometimes called [**units** or **formations**](/wiki/Military_organization#Commands,_formations,_and_units "Military organization"), form the building blocks of a military. A commander is normally specifically appointed to the role in order to provide a [legal framework](/wiki/Legal_doctrine "Legal doctrine") for the authority bestowed. [Naval](/wiki/Naval "Naval") and [military](/wiki/Military "Military") officers have legal authority by virtue of their [officer's commission](/wiki/Commissioned_officer "Commissioned officer"), but the specific responsibilities and privileges of command are derived from the publication of appointment.

The relevant definition of 'command' according to the [United States Department of Defense](/wiki/United_States_Department_of_Defense "United States Department of Defense") (US DOD) is as follows:[[1]](#cite_note-1)

> (DOD) 3. A unit or units, an organization, or an area under the command of one individual. Also called CMD. See also area command; [combatant command](/wiki/Combatant_command "Combatant command"); combatant command (command authority).

## Major Command

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Command_(military_formation)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Major Command")]

Major Command or Major Commands are large [formations](/wiki/Military_organization#Commands,_formations,_and_units "Military organization") used by the [United States Department of Defense](/wiki/United_States_Department_of_Defense "United States Department of Defense"). Historically, a Major Command is the highest level of command. Within the [United States Army](/wiki/United_States_Army "United States Army"), the acronym MACOM is used for Major Command.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Within the [United States Air Force](/wiki/United_States_Air_Force "United States Air Force") (USAF), the acronym MAJCOM is used.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

There are several types of DOD major commands:

* [List of major commands of the United States Air Force](/wiki/List_of_major_commands_of_the_United_States_Air_Force "List of major commands of the United States Air Force")
* [List of major commands of the United States Army](/wiki/List_of_major_commands_of_the_United_States_Army "List of major commands of the United States Army")
* [Naval Education and Training Command](/wiki/Naval_Education_and_Training_Command "Naval Education and Training Command") of the [United States Navy](/wiki/United_States_Navy "United States Navy")
* Special operations commands such as [Special Operations Command Europe](/wiki/Special_Operations_Command_Europe "Special Operations Command Europe")
* Unified commands overseas such as [Multinational Force Iraq](/wiki/Multinational_Force_Iraq "Multinational Force Iraq"); [United States Forces Iraq](/wiki/United_States_Forces_Iraq "United States Forces Iraq") or [Combined Security Transition Command - Afghanistan](/wiki/Combined_Security_Transition_Command_-_Afghanistan "Combined Security Transition Command - Afghanistan")
